zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Piers Peter Braose Brewse Lord of Winston於1310年出生在英國英格蘭素可維斯頓時，他的父親William de Braose 2nd Baron Braose50歲，母親Agnes de Sully47歲。他於1341年與Joan de Percy結婚。他於1350年住在英國素福爾可。他於1346年登記服兵役。他於1364年在英國被列為knight of king edward's chamber。他去世於1378年10月4日之前在英國英格蘭素可維斯頓，埋葬在英國英格蘭素可維斯頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Piers Peter Braose Brewse Lord of Winston于1310年出生在英国英格兰素可維斯頓时，他的父亲William de Braose 2nd Baron Braose50岁，母亲Agnes de Sully47岁。他于1341年与Joan de Percy结婚。他于1350年住在英国素福爾可。他于1346年登记服兵役。他于1364年在英国被列为knight of king edward's chamber。他去世于1378年10月4日之前在英国英格兰素可維斯頓，埋葬在英国英格兰素可維斯頓。

nameMeanings.name: Braose
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Eckhard, Gerhard, Manfred, Otto.
from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Ambrosius (see Ambrose ).

variants.name: Ambrose
meanings: English and Scottish: from the personal name Ambrose (French Ambroise, Latin Ambrosius, from Greek ambrosios ‘immortal, divine’), which was popular throughout Christendom in medieval Europe. Its popularity was due in part to the fame of Saint Ambrose (c. 340–397), one of the four Latin Fathers of the Church, the teacher of Saint Augustine. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. Czech, Slovenian, and Croatian Ambrož (see Ambroz ), and also their derivatives, e.g. the Slovenian patronymic Ambrožič.
Irish: from Mac Ambróis ‘son of Ambrose’ (see 1 above); a West Munster name, which has also been Anglicized as McCambridge .

nameMeanings.name: Brewse
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Montgomeryshire; also Norfolk): nickname for an irritating person, from Middle English brese ‘gadfly’ (Old English brēosa). See also Breeze , compare Breece .

German: habitational name from any of numerous places called Breese or Breesen, in Mecklenburg, Wendland (near Hanover), Brandenburg, and Pomerania. In some cases the placename is derived from a Slavic word meaning ‘birch’ (brěza in Old Slavic).

variants.name: Brese
meanings: metonymic occupational name from Middle Low German brēse ‘brooch, needle, clasp’, denoting a maker of such items.

variants.name: Breece
meanings: English (Montgomeryshire and Denbighshire): variant of   Breese  .

variants.name: Breeze
meanings: English (Shropshire and Staffordshire; also Norfolk, Montgomeryshire, and Lancashire): variant of Breese . The name migrated from Shropshire into Montgomeryshire. Some dictionaries derive it from the Welsh patronymic ab Rhys, but this is phonetically improbable, as ab would develop to ap before the voiceless r of Rhys, hence Preece . Compare Breece .

nameMeanings.name: Piers
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Regular medieval vernacular form of Peter (from the Old French nominative case, as against the oblique Pier, modern Pierre). In the form Pierce it survived into the 18th century, although in part this may be a transferred use of the surname derived from the medieval given name. Piers was revived in the mid-20th century, perhaps partly under the influence of William Langland's great rambling medieval poem Piers Plowman ( 1367–86 ), in which the character of Piers symbolizes the virtues of hard work, honesty, and fairness.

nameMeanings.name: Peter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name of the best-known of all Christ's apostles, traditionally regarded as the founder of the Christian Church. The name derives, via Latin, from Greek petros ‘stone, rock’. This is used as a translation of the Aramaic byname Cephas, given to the apostle Simon son of Jona, to distinguish him from another of the same name ( Simon Zelotes ). ‘When Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone’ (John 1:42). According to Matthew 16:17–18, Christ says more explicitly, ‘Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona … thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church’. The name has been in continuous use since the Middle Ages.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Piers Peter Braose Brewse Lord of Winston was born in 1310, in Wiston, Sussex, England, United Kingdom, his father, William de Braose 2nd Baron Braose, was 50 and his mother, Agnes de Sully, was 47. He married Joan de Percy in 1341. He lived in Suffolk, England in 1350. He registered for military service in 1346. In 1364, his occupation is listed as knight of king edward's chamber in England. He died before 4 October 1378, in Wiston, Sussex, England, United Kingdom, and was buried in Wiston, Sussex, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Piers Peter Braose Brewse Lord of Winston est né en 1310, à Wiston, Sussex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, William de Braose 2nd Baron Braose, avait 50 ans et sa mère, Agnes de Sully, avait 47 ans. Il a épousé Joan de Percy en 1341. Il a vécu à Suffolk, Angleterre en 1350. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1346. En 1364, sa profession de son indiquée est knight of king edward's chamber à Angleterre. Il est décédé avant 4 octobre 1378, à Wiston, Sussex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, et a été enterré à Wiston, Sussex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Piers Peter Braose Brewse Lord of Winston 1310 in Wiston, Sussex, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater William de Braose 2nd Baron Braose 50 und seine Mutter Agnes de Sully 47. Er hat 1341 Joan de Percy geheiratet. 1350 hat er in Suffolk, England gelebt. Er hat sich 1346 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. 1364 ist sein Beruf als knight of king edward's chamber in England aufgeführt. Er starb vor 4. Oktober 1378 in Wiston, Sussex, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Wiston, Sussex, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Piers Peter Braose Brewse Lord of Winston è nato il nel 1310 nel Wiston, Sussex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, William de Braose 2nd Baron Braose, aveva 50 anni e il suo madre, Agnes de Sully, aveva 47 anni. Lui sposato Joan de Percy nel 1341. Vissuto a Suffolk, England nel 1350. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1346. Nel 1364, la sua occupazione è indicata come knight of king edward's chamber nel England. Morì prima del 4 ottobre 1378, nel Wiston, Sussex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, e fu sepolto a Wiston, Sussex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Piers Peter Braose Brewse Lord of Winstonが1310年にイギリスイギリススセックスウィストンで生まれた時，父William de Braose 2nd Baron Braoseは50歳，母Agnes de Sullyは47歳であった。1341年にJoan de Percyと結婚した。彼は1350年にはイギリススフォルクに住んでいた。彼は1346年に兵役登録をした。1364年，イギリスでの職業はknight of king edward's chamberと記録されている。彼は1378年10月4日以前にイギリスイギリススセックスウィストンで死去し，イギリスイギリススセックスウィストンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Piers Peter Braose Brewse Lord of Winston(이)가 1310년 영국 잉글랜드 수세스 와이스턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William de Braose 2nd Baron Braose(은)는 50세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes de Sully(은)는 47세였다. 그는 1341년 Joan de Percy(와)과 결혼했다. 1350년도에 영국 수폴크에서 살았다. 그는 1346년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 1364년, 그의 직업은 영국에서 knight of king edward's chamber이었다. 그는 1378년 10월 4일 이전 영국 잉글랜드 수세스 와이스턴에서 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 수세스 와이스턴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Piers Peter Braose Brewse Lord of Winston nasceu em 1310, em Wiston, Sussex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, William de Braose 2nd Baron Braose, tinha 50 anos e sua mãe, Agnes de Sully, tinha 47 anos. Ele casou-se com Joan de Percy em 1341. Ele viveu em Suffolk, Inglaterra em 1350. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1346. Em 1364, sua profissão aparece como knight of king edward's chamber em Inglaterra. Ele faleceu antes 4 de outubro de 1378, em Wiston, Sussex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, e foi sepultado em Wiston, Sussex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Piers Peter Braose Brewse Lord of Winston родился в 1310 г. в местности вистон, суссекс, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени William de Braose 2nd Baron Braose было 50 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes de Sully, 47. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan de Percy в 1341 г. Он жил в местности суффолк, Англия в 1350 году. В 1346 году он поступил на военную службу. В 1364 годуработал по профессии knight of king edward's chamber в местности Англия. Он умер перед 4 октябрь 1378 г. в местности вистон, суссекс, Англия, Великобритания и был похоронен в местности вистон, суссекс, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Piers Peter Braose Brewse Lord of Winston nació en 1310, en Wiston, Sussex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, William de Braose 2nd Baron Braose, tenía 50 años y su mamá, Agnes de Sully, tenía 47 años. Se casó con Joan de Percy en 1341. Vivió en Suffolk, Inglaterra en 1350. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1346. En 1364, su ocupación registrada era knight of king edward's chamber en Inglaterra. Murió antes de 4 de octubre de 1378, en Wiston, Sussex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, y fue sepultado en Wiston, Sussex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
